prompt: Wide architectural eye-level view of the finished Salem NH primary bath, showing both the freestanding tub and the walk-in glass shower in one frame. Walls clad in 24x48 large-format porcelain tile in a soft greige stone-look. Cast-iron freestanding tub center-left, brushed-bronze floor-mount filler. Glass walk-in shower right with rain-head and hand-shower in matching brushed bronze. Floating walnut double vanity below a long backlit mirror. Heated herringbone porcelain floor. Shot with a 24mm wide architectural lens, dusk warm tungsten light from sconces and a recessed cove, calm and quiet. A small red Anjo-style ceramic mug on the vanity as the only saturated color. High-contrast slightly desaturated palette, NOT HGTV bright, NOT luxury staged. Documentary, working-contractor catalog feel. No readable text, no logos.
